summary: This notebook demonstrates a workflow for processing multimodal data using BigQuery AI and the OpenAI API. It shows how to generate text embeddings, upload them to BigQuery, create a vector index, and perform similarity searches directly in SQL. The author also illustrates how to leverage BigQuery's native AI functions to automatically generate summaries for retrieved documents. Readers should learn how to integrate external embedding APIs with cloud-native vector search and generative AI capabilities to build scalable data processing pipelines.
competition_flow: Generates text embeddings via OpenAI, loads them into BigQuery, creates a vector index, performs similarity search, and generates summaries using BigQuery AI functions.
data_reading: Hardcoded text list converted to a pandas DataFrame and uploaded to BigQuery using `client.load_table_from_dataframe`.
data_processing: None explicitly shown; relies on API-generated embeddings and BigQuery's native vector indexing.
features_engineering: Text embeddings generated via OpenAI API (`text-embedding-ada-002`).
insights: BigQuery AI natively supports vector similarity search and text generation without requiring external ML frameworks.
External embedding APIs like OpenAI can be easily integrated into BigQuery workflows for scalable data indexing.
SQL-based pipelines can automate the entire process from embedding generation to document summarization.
